text: **Anna:** Winter {weather|天候/気象} in Washington DC can be really {windy|風の強い|/ˈwɪndi/|It is windy today}.
**Anna:** It is really {windy|風の強い} today.
**Anna:** Will it be {windy|風の強い} this weekend?
**Anna:** I'll {listen|聞く/傾聴する} to the news.
**Anna:** I am tired of my {untidy|乱れた/整っていない} hair.
**Pete:** Are you tired of your {untidy|乱れた} hair?
**Pete:** And I have the {perfect|完璧な/理想的な} product for you.
**Pete:** Just spray Hair Be Good on your hair.
**Anna:** in a towel and you are done.
**Anna:** Hair be good works and it's {inexpensive|安価な}.
**Anna:** So go online and {order|注文する} your Hair Be Good today.
**Anna:** And I can {trust|信頼する} Pete.
**Anna:** I am buying some right now.
**Anna:** Okay, I will order one can of Hair Be Good for $10.
**Anna:** I'll pay with a {credit card|クレジットカード}.
**Anna:** My {delivery|配達} will arrive in two days.
**Anna:** I'll use it before I go to {work|仕事|/wɜːrk/|I go to work every day.}.
**Anna:** Okay, hair be good, make my {untidy|乱れた/整理されていない|/ʌnˈtaɪdi/|My room is very untidy.} hair {tidy|きちんとした/整頓された|/ˈtaɪdi/|Please keep your desk tidy.}.
**Anna:** No, make it {fabulous|素晴らしい/素晴ら|/ˈfæbjələs/|She looks fabulous tonight.}.
**Amelia:** Anna, you look {different|違う/異なる|/ˈdɪfərənt/|You look different today.}.
**Anna:** I used a new {product|製品/商品|/ˈprɒdʌkt/|This is a new product.}.
**Amelia:** Your hair does not look good.
**Amelia:** Why don't you go home and take care of this?
**Pete:** Is something {wrong|悪い/間違っている|/rɔːŋ/|Something is wrong with my computer.}?
**Pete:** What did you do to your hair?
**Anna:** Hair be good did this to my hair.
**Anna:** You said in your ad it was a {perfect|完璧な/完全な|/ˈpɜːrfɪkt/|This is a perfect solution.} product.
**Anna:** This is {imperfect|不完全な/欠陥のある|/ɪmˈpɜːrfɪkt/|The product is imperfect.}
**Anna:** It's {unbelievable|信じられない|/ˌʌnbɪˈliːvəbl/|This story is unbelievable.}.
**Anna:** It was {dishonest|正直でない/不誠実|/dɪsˈɒnɪst/|He was dishonest with his friends}.
**Pete:** Anna, you don't believe everything you see in {abs|腹筋/腹部|/æbz/|He works out to get six-pack abs}, do you?
**Anna:** Just the ones with my friends.
**Pete:** Just wash it a {couple|2つの/いくつかの|/ˈkʌpəl/|A couple of days ago.}
**Anna:** Here, here's your {product|製品/商品|/ˈprɒdʌkt/|This is a new product.}
**Anna:** You should call it {hair|髪の毛|/heə(r)/|My hair is long.} be really really bad.
